---
name: discovery
depth: Standard
keywords: []
description: Explore and validate an idea before deciding to build it (opt-in)
---

# discovery scope

An opt-in scope that runs ideation as an idea-to-decision flow: build an
evidenced picture of the current state, choose a problem framing from
genuinely distinct candidates, express the future state as options, test the
riskiest assumptions before any commitment, and end in an explicit commit,
pivot, or park decision over a decision pack.

## Why these stages

`discovery` executes the 3 initialization stages (they always run), the
existing `intent-capture` stage — extended for every scope with
materials-first intake, so discovery needs no intent stage of its own — and
the four discovery stages in ideation: `discovery-current-state`,
`discovery-future-state`, `discovery-experimentation`, and
`discovery-decision`. Every other stage is SKIP: the six questionnaire-driven
ideation stages that follow intent-capture are the existing implementation
this scope offers an alternative to, and the inception, construction, and
operation phases belong to a delivery scope that a committed initiative
graduates into. The phase boundary is the decision gate. On commit the
person chooses where the build happens: continue in this workspace, where
the decision stage relays the engine's own scope change so the same workflow
and record flow straight into delivery, or hand off to another repository or
team, where the decision pack's handoff contract is what the receiving
workflow starts from. A park keeps the record intact and resumable.

## Membership

`discovery` has no keyword triggers (`keywords: []`), the same convention
composed scopes use, so it never captures freeform routing that belongs to
the stock scopes. It is reachable only by explicit selection: `/aidlc
discovery` or `/aidlc --scope discovery`. The `feature` fallback and the
compose offer are unaffected.

## Depth

Standard depth. The required open-questions entries for a run are exactly
the answers its decision gate consumes, so a small initiative legitimately
runs a short record. Evidence rules do not relax with depth: a claim without
a source is an assumption, and analysis with no execution caps at
inconclusive.
